rationale: Hard pass for aquapark.io. The dark navy/yellow crime-scene palette and somber nighttime forensic imagery directly clash with the game's bright, sunny, cartoonish water-park identity, and a police investigation hook has zero connective tissue to sliding/bumping/racing mechanics. The mature, true-crime curiosity audience also skews far older and more dramatic than the 7-25 hypercasual racer demo — only viable if reworked into a wildly off-brand "who pushed the player off the slide?" parody, which isn't worth the production risk given the single-creative, low-evidence cluster.
